name: Xenogeneic Mitochondrial Transplantation
summary: Preclinical transplantation of mitochondria across species boundaries, tracked separately because donor equivalence, immune compatibility, and quality control are central limitations.
mechanism_summary: The approach supplies donor mitochondria from a different species with the goal of improving host mitochondrial function and age-associated phenotypes, while exposing source-compatibility, respiratory-competence, immunogenicity, and durability questions.

open_questions: Whether benefits replicate with other donor species or donor preparations
Whether mitochondrial respiratory competence predicts in vivo efficacy across tissues
Whether cross-species donor mitochondria raise unacceptable immune, germline, or long-term safety risks
Whether age-associated phenotype movement is durable and functionally meaningful beyond short animal windows
